channel.title: Alejandro W. Cuatrecasas Schmitz
channel.description: Recent content on Alejandro W. Cuatrecasas Schmitz

item.title: Too Small To Fail
item.description:
The second day my wife and I arrived in Raleigh, we saw it painted on a wall. Four words. We stopped. We looked at each other. We didn’t need to say anything.

Too small to fail.

I’ve been carrying those words since. This is my attempt to explain why.

BTW: I’m writing here for the same reason I do most things: I need structure to understand, and I need to understand to remember. This is my corner outside my head — a place to shape thoughts, ask questions, and share things worth the time. This is my first post.

item.title: About
item.description:
I’m Alejandro. Curious by nature — I like understanding things deeply, and I look for the simple patterns that connect the world: in science, in art, in people, and in everyday life.

Having clear goals helps me turn noise into direction — and I’d rather find a different path than follow the obvious one.

I believe in simplicity as a form of excellence, in doing the small things well, and in the quiet presence of beautiful things.

item.title: Good stuff
item.description: Work in progress — a living list of things worth your time.

item.title: Questions
item.description: Work in progress — things I keep asking myself.
